title: Use a consistent corner radius system
summary: AI-generated interfaces apply different border-radius values to similar component types, making the shape language feel arbitrary.
instruction: Define 2-3 border-radius tokens mapped to component roles (e.g., sharp for data display, rounded for interactive, pill for badges). Apply the same radius to all components of the same type.
rationale: When cards, inputs, buttons, and modals all have different radii (8px, 6px, 4px, 12px), the shape language becomes noise. Users unconsciously associate roundedness with approachability and sharpness with precision - inconsistency creates mixed signals.
violations: A form where inputs have 4px radius, buttons have 8px, the form container has 12px, and error alerts have 6px - four different radii within one logical section.
A card grid where each card has a different radius because the AI applied radius values inconsistently during generation.
controls: A component set with two radii: 4px for all inputs, cards, and panels (functional surface), 8px for all buttons and interactive elements.
An editorial layout using 0px radius on all containers with 2px radius only on pull-quote borders.
borderlineCases: A hero section may use a larger radius on the main container (e.g., 24px) while interior components use smaller radii. This is acceptable if it follows a defined token system.
contextualExceptions: Single-radius designs where every component uses the same radius are not affected.
Design systems where radius is inherited from a parent component.
detectorSignal: More than 3 distinct border-radius values applied to similar component types
matcherProposal: Group CSS elements by component type heuristic (div.card, section, form input, button). Collect border-radius values. If the same component type uses 3+ different radii, flag. Also flag if the project uses 5+ total distinct radii without a clear scale pattern.
detectorLimitations: Responsive radius changes (larger radius on mobile, smaller on desktop) may produce multiple values per component.
Border-radius shorthand with 2-4 values creates different radii per corner intentionally.
falsePositiveScenarios: A component library with defined radius tokens but only using 2 of them in practice - the unused tokens should not trigger detection.
Border-radius: 9999px (pill) on badges alongside 8px on buttons is intentional 2-radius system.
mitigationStrategy: Count radii per component type, not globally. A project with 5 total radii that are consistently mapped to 5 component roles (card=8px, input=4px, button=8px, badge=pill, modal=12px) is fine.

fixturePlans.violation: A page where all cards use different radii: 6px, 8px, 10px, 12px, 16px across 5 cards in the same grid.
fixturePlans.control: A page where cards=8px, buttons=8px, inputs=4px, badges=9999px (3 distinct roles with consistent values per role).
fixturePlans.borderline: A page where the hero card has 24px radius (intentional hero treatment) while section cards are 8px - two-value system.

cleanRoomAttestations: Original observation. Consistent radius is standard design system practice.
overlapNotes: Related to radius.everything-pill (which flags universal pill rounding). This is about radius inconsistency rather than radius magnitude. Complementary - one targets excessive rounding, the other targets inconsistent rounding.
